questionText: Objective 1 (Recognize names of gadgets): Which of the following is a gadget used for communication?
explanation: A smartphone is a gadget used for communication. A fridge and washing machine are appliances, and a television is used for entertainment.

questionText: Objective 1 (Recognize names of gadgets): Which of these is an appliance used in the kitchen?
explanation: A fridge is a kitchen appliance used to keep food cold. Laptops, tablets, and smartphones are gadgets or devices.

questionText: Objective 1 (Recognize names of gadgets): What is the name of the device shown in this description: "It is a portable computer with a touchscreen, smaller than a laptop."
explanation: A tablet is a portable computer with a touchscreen, smaller than a laptop. Smartphones are smaller and used primarily for communication.

questionText: Objective 1 (Recognize names of gadgets): Which appliance is used to wash clothes?
explanation: A washing machine is an appliance used to wash clothes. A fridge keeps food cold, television is for entertainment, and a laptop is a computer.

questionText: Objective 2 (Read brochures, captions, and simple presentations): You see a caption under a picture: "This device has a keyboard and a screen. It can be used for work, school, and entertainment." What is being described?
explanation: A laptop has a keyboard and a screen and is used for work, school, and entertainment. Smartphones and tablets have touchscreens and not always a physical keyboard.

questionText: Objective 2 (Read brochures, captions, and simple presentations): Read the following presentation: "The Samsung Galaxy is a popular smartphone. It has a large screen and a high-quality camera." What is the topic of this presentation?
explanation: The presentation mentions "Samsung Galaxy" and "smartphone". The topic is clearly a smartphone.

questionText: Objective 2 (Read brochures, captions, and simple presentations): You read a brochure about a product: "This appliance keeps your food fresh for longer periods of time." What is the product?
explanation: A fridge keeps food fresh for longer periods of time. A washing machine washes clothes, a laptop is a computer, and a television is for entertainment.

questionText: Objective 3 (Draw and label pictures of gadgets and appliances): You are asked to draw and label a device used for washing dishes. Which of these should you draw?
explanation: A dishwasher is an appliance used for washing dishes. A fridge keeps food cold, a Smart TV is for entertainment, and a tablet is a portable computer.

questionText: Objective 3 (Draw and label pictures of gadgets and appliances): Your teacher asks you to draw a gadget that you can carry in your pocket and use to make calls. What should you draw?
explanation: A smartphone is a gadget you can carry in your pocket and use to make calls. Laptops are bigger, and fridges and washing machines are large appliances.

questionText: Objective 3 (Draw and label pictures of gadgets and appliances): Which of the following should you NOT label as a gadget?
explanation: A washing machine is an appliance, not typically considered a gadget. Smart TVs, tablets, and smartphones are all gadgets.

questionText: Objective 1 (Recognize names of gadgets): Which device is used to watch movies, play games, and browse the internet on a large screen?
explanation: A Smart TV is used to watch movies, play games, and browse the internet on a large screen. Smartphones are smaller, and washing machines and fridges are appliances.

questionText: Objective 2 (Read brochures, captions, and simple presentations): You read a caption: "This gadget allows you to listen to music, check social media, and take photos." What is being described?
explanation: A smartphone allows you to listen to music, check social media, and take photos. A laptop can do these too, but the description is more typical of a smartphone.

questionText: Objective 3 (Draw and label pictures): You want to draw and label a device that is used to keep food frozen. What should you draw?
explanation: A freezer is specifically used to keep food frozen. A fridge keeps food cold, an oven is for cooking, and a microwave is for reheating food.

questionText: Objective 2 (Read simple presentations): You read: "The iPad is a popular tablet. It has a long battery life and is used for reading and watching videos." What is the main function of the iPad?
explanation: The presentation states that the iPad is used for reading and watching videos. Making calls is a function of a smartphone, washing clothes is for a washing machine, and keeping food cold is for a fridge.

questionText: Objective 1 (Recognize names of gadgets/appliances): Which of the following is NOT an appliance?
explanation: A laptop is a computer (gadget), not an appliance. Fridges, washing machines, and televisions are considered appliances.
